content.full_text:
Scott Halabrin
Acting General Counsel
FPC
428 J. Street  Suite 600
Sacramento, CA.  95814

Dear Mr. Halabrin:

I am getting conflicting advise over the telephone from the advisors at the FPC.  Therefore, I am writing to you, as advised today by one of your advisors.

I am running, as a private individual, a petition drive to get a referendum on the November ballot.  I want to reverse the decision of the county supervisors in regard to approving a large subdivision in the Ione foothills.  If we gather enough signatures (1273), the supervisors will either have to reverse their decision or put the issue on the ballot, presumably in November.  The supervisors approved the project on February 11, 1992.

My question is do I have to fill out any of your disclosure forms?

I am using my own money for the most part, but I have collected a little from other sources.

Thanks for your help.


Sincerely,


Mel Booker

Box 566
Challenge, CA.
95925

673-2282

[This appears to be a very faded or low contrast document image where the text is largely illegible. The image shows what seems to be a typed document with multiple paragraphs, but the text is too faint or unclear to accurately transcribe the specific words and content. Without being able to reliably read the text, I cannot provide an accurate transcription while following the requirement to transcribe exactly what is seen.]
